gaps: fedcourts query has no docket/case-id filter, so I could not pull this case's own corpus row (e.g. to check predict_excluded or topic) without stepping outside the documented tooling
corpus topic tags are empty for the historical SCOTUS set, so topic-filtered priors and stats return nothing
a disposition-coding guide for pre-1925 merits appeals (what the reconciler maps affirm/reverse/modify onto) would remove the main source of prediction uncertainty
helpful: fedcourts stats --group-by disposition (era base rates were the calibration anchor)
fedcourts open-events (confirmed the event is open)
notes: Snapshot was nearly empty, so corpus base rates did most of the work. fedcourts query with --topic and --citation filters returned no usable priors for this case.
